title: Viidaav (species)
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Viidaav_warriors.jpg]] |name=Viidaav |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]{{CWACite|5|Heroes on Both Sides (comic story)}} |class= |subspecies= |races= |height=About 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]The Viidaavs of "[[Heroes on Both Sides (comic story)|''Heroes on Both Sides'']]" stand roughly the same height as [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]], who are 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall according to {{SWE|groups|clonetroopers|clone troopers}}. |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=*[[Color/Legends|Green]] *Orange{{CWACite|7|Spy Girls}} |haircolor=*Green *Orange |feathers= |eyecolor=Red |distinctions=Squared-off, spikey projections on head |lifespan= |origin=[[Viidaav]]{{CSWECite|III|306}} |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|Commander, the Viidaav won't be able to evacuate in time, either. They're killing themselves—and their own planet.''"
"''It's more likely their Separatist 'allies' triggered it. Probably using the colony's own mining charges, buried in the crust. Order the other units to cover us. I doubt the Viidaav even know the signal's been activated… and they aren't in the mood to listen to us anyway.|A Republic clone trooper and clone commander, during the battle on Viidaav|}} The '''Viidaav''', or '''Viidaavs''', were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with [[Color/Legends|green]] or orange skin and angular projections growing from their heads. They were thin beings with large, expressive [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and three-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]]. The Viidaavs hailed from the eponymous [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Viidaav]], where many of them worked as [[Miner/Legends|miners]], [[Starshipwright|shipbuilders]], and [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Viidaavs allied with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. [[Clone trooper/Legends|Clone troopers]] from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] attacked their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], sparking a [[Battle of Viidaav|battle]] on the planet. The natives [[Ground warfare/Legends|resisted]] the Republic [[Troop/Legends|troops]], but were eventually driven back to defending a small [[mining array]]. The Viidaavs were preparing to retreat when Separatist [[Head of State/Legends|leader]] [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] ordered his [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] to detonate [[Mining charge/Legends|mining charges]] set within the planet's crust. A lone [[Unidentified Viidaav commander|Viidaav commander]] managed to defuse the explosives [[Standard second|seconds]] before they exploded. ==Biology and appearance== Viidaavs (or Viidaav)Both plural forms are used in the story "[[Heroes on Both Sides (comic story)|''Heroes on Both Sides'']]." were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] bipeds with [[Color/Legends|green]] or orange skin and lithe, [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] bodies. Their [[Limb/Legends|feet]] had two large toes, and their hands had three thick fingers with nails. The average Viidaav stood about the same height as [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]], a group who were 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. The Viidaav head was covered in thick, square-like projections. They had large, red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] under an expressive brow, and white [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] behind thick, green lips. Their prominent [[Bone/Legends|cheekbones]] framed small, round [[Ear/Legends|ears]] on the sides of the head. Members of the [[Species/Legends|species]] had no visible [[Nose/Legends|nose]]. ==Society and culture== [[File:Viidaav_running.jpg|thumb|left|Viidaavs had three-fingered hands and thin builds.]] The species lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Viidaav]], located in the [[Viidaav system/Legends|Viidaav system]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Their population centers were situated near large outcrops of stone and had only small numbers of settlers. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], they had access to [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]]-standard technology, including [[Macrobinoculars/Legends|macrobinoculars]] and [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]]. These rifles were gray with a long, wide [[Barrel/Legends|barrel]] and a shoulder mount. These [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] were used in combat against [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] during the [[Battle of Viidaav]]. They proved to be a match for the [[Cloning/Legends|clones]]' [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|DC-15A blaster rifles]]. At least some members of the species worked as [[Miner/Legends|miners]], and the Viidaav operated at least one [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Unidentified Viidaav colony|colony]] on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld's]] surface."[[Heroes on Both Sides (comic story)|''Heroes on Both Sides'']]" is ambiguous about whether the "Viidaav mining colony" is a planet colonized by the Viidaavs or an area of their homeworld colonized for mining. {{CSWECite|III|306}} indicates that the colony existed on the Viidaav homeworld, Viidaav. There, they used [[Mining charge/Legends|mining charges]] under the planetary crust to help excavate valuable materials. Other Viidaavs worked as [[starshipwright]]s and [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. During the Clone Wars, Viidaav soldiers wore uniforms that consisted of a brown [[baldric]] over the left shoulder, brown [[Boot/Legends|boots]], a brown loincloth, and brown wraps around the forearms. Soldiers bore a colored stripe on the chin, and at least one [[Commander/Legends|commander]] wore white face paint around the eyes and the center of the forehead. Meanwhile, Viidaav civilians wore clothing standard to other members of the [[galactic community]]. ==History== {{Quote|Activate the detonation sequence. I expect the planet's surface to be an inferno before I leave the system.''"
"''And the Viidaav colonists?''"
"''We need them to keep the Republic troops pinned down—let them live on in history.|Grievous explains his trap to a B1 battle droid|}} The Viidaavs evolved on the planet named for them, which was located in the [[Bryx sector/Legends|Bryx sector]]{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-sectors-of-the-mid-rim|text=Essential Atlas Extra: Sectors of the Mid Rim}} of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. They spread across their homeworld and founded at least one colony on the planet, which was used for mining. As part of mining operations, the Viidaav colonists set explosive charges into the planetary crust for help in the excavation of raw materials. The species also set up a shipbuilding industry on the world. [[File:Viidaav_servers.png|thumb|right|Viidaav servers worked the crowd at Bezz Drexx's tower on Coruscant.]] Their planet lay in a region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] known as [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]], which first came to the attention of beings from another world by [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]]. In the [[Jedi Civil War]] of [[3959 BBY|3959]] to [[3956 BBY]], Viidaav fell within the sphere of influence of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan's]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. At some point during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] and subsequent Clone Wars, the Viidaavs sided with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] rather than the Galactic Republic. In the early days of hostilities between the factions, the Viidaavs provided [[Starship/Legends|starships]] to the Confederacy while the Separatists sent [[Army/Legends|armies]] of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] and helped set up a base and command center in a mining colony. During the same period, three Viidaavs served as wait staff for Separatist-sympathizer [[Bezz Drexx]] at a [[Bezz Drexx's tower|tower]] on the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. ===Battle on Viidaav=== {{Main|Battle of Viidaav}} {{Quote|Has the Republic landed most of their troops?''"
"''Enough to set the trap.''"
"''And our Viidaav allies?''"
"''A few hundred thousand remain, but we do not have enough ships to take them with us.''"
"''Then they should have built us more in the time they were given.|Grievous and Dooku discuss the battle on Viidaav|}} [[File:BattleOfViidaav.jpg|thumb|left|During the battle with Republic forces, Viidaav soldiers defended a mining array from Galactic Republic clone troopers.]] After Republic [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] found the Separatist base on Viidaav in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] attacked the world, and a [[Battle of Viidaav|battle]] began. The natives and their [[Droid/Legends|droid]] allies [[Ground warfare/Legends|resisted]], but the Republic landed most of its available forces and moved ever closer toward the Viidaav mining colony. There, Viidaav soldiers were reduced to a few hundred thousand [[Troop/Legends|troops]] concentrated around the defense of a small [[mining array]] in a bunker. With the Republic poised to take the array and the world, the Viidaavs prepared to evacuate the planet. Any retreat had to rely on spacecraft of Viidaav manufacture, but too few remained for a full evacuation. Meanwhile, the Confederate [[Head of State/Legends|leader]], [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], was secretly plotting to take out large numbers of Republic clone troopers, but the plan relied on a sizable [[Death/Legends|loss]] of Viidaav lives as well and would leave the planet's surface uninhabitable: Dooku ordered his battle droids to surreptitiously set the mining charges in the planet's crust to destruct, and he left the Viidaav forces to fight on to keep the Republic forces engaged until the explosives went off. The battle droids set the charges and initiated the bunker's defense systems to slow down attempts to disable it. The Viidaavs recognized that they had been tricked and sent a unit to defuse the explosives. At the same time, a unit of clone troopers approached the bunker, and the two sides exchanged fire and suffered heavy casualties. Eventually, only a single member remained on both sides: the [[Unidentified Viidaav commander|Viidaav commander]] and the [[Unidentified clone trooper commander (Viidaav)|clone commander]]. Both entered the mining array from different entrances. The enemies fought through the bunker's defenses, met in the [[Computer/Legends|computer]] room, and leveled their weapons at one another; the computer steadily counted down. With three seconds remaining, the Viidaav commander fired his weapon and was shot dead. Only then did the clone realize that the green-skinned warrior had only intended to shoot the computer. At any rate, the computer was destroyed, the explosives deactivated, and the battle finished. ===Later history=== After the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] splintered following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Viidaavs and their homeworld came under the domain of an Imperial splinter faction territory known as the [[Greater Maldrood]], a situation that persisted at least until [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]. By [[137 ABY]], their world was within the confines of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]]. ==Behind the scenes== The Viidaavs appear in "[[Heroes on Both Sides (comic story)|''Heroes on Both Sides'']]," a story first published in the [[2006]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 5]]''. The story was written by [[Chris Avellone]] and illustrated by [[Stewart McKenny]] and [[Dan Jackson]]. The comic leaves it vague whether the world depicted is the Viidaav homeworld or a colony. However, the [[2008]] book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' clarifies the matter, giving the name "Viidaav" to both the species and the planet depicted in the story. The [[2009]] source ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the world in the Mid Rim. Viidaav characters also appear in the [[2007]] McKenney story "''[[Spy Girls]]''," part of the comic book ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 7]]''. ==Appearances== *{{CWACite|5|Heroes on Both Sides (comic story)}} {{1st}} *{{CWACite|7|Spy Girls}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Viidaav (species)}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Viidaav (Species) }} [[Category:Mid Rim sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Viidaavs]] }}
